Patch Set 5:

I'm not sure why this is failing now, the only difference is how the PID is collected:

 CMD: trevis-98vm2 $RUNAS unshare -Um sleep 600 &>/dev/null & echo \$!
 CMD: trevis-98vm2 ps auxww
 root       15777  0.0  0.0   4728   800 ?        S    23:10   0:00 sleep 600
 Created NS: child (sleep) pid=15777 in 0 seconds
 CMD: trevis-98vm2 runas -u 500 -g 500 newuidmap 15777 0 500 1
 trevis-98vm2: running as uid/gid/euid/egid 500/500/500/500, groups:
 trevis-98vm2:  [newuidmap] [15777] [0] [500] [1]
 trevis-98vm2: newuidmap: Target process 15777 is owned by a different user: uid:500 pw_uid:500 st_uid:0, gid:500 pw_gid:500 st_gid:0
 pdsh@trevis-98vm1: trevis-98vm2: ssh exited with exit code 1
 sanity-pcc test_101a: @@@@@@ FAIL: could not map uid 500 to root in namespace

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> I'm not sure why this is failing now, the only difference is how the PID is collected:
> 
>  CMD: trevis-98vm2 $RUNAS unshare -Um sleep 600 &>/dev/null & echo \$!
>  CMD: trevis-98vm2 ps auxww
>  root       15777  0.0  0.0   4728   800 ?        S    23:10   0:00 sleep 600
>  Created NS: child (sleep) pid=15777 in 0 seconds
>  CMD: trevis-98vm2 runas -u 500 -g 500 newuidmap 15777 0 500 1
>  trevis-98vm2: running as uid/gid/euid/egid 500/500/500/500, groups:
>  trevis-98vm2:  [newuidmap] [15777] [0] [500] [1]
>  trevis-98vm2: newuidmap: Target process 15777 is owned by a different user: uid:500 pw_uid:500 st_uid:0, gid:500 pw_gid:500 st_gid:0
>  pdsh@trevis-98vm1: trevis-98vm2: ssh exited with exit code 1
>  sanity-pcc test_101a: @@@@@@ FAIL: could not map uid 500 to root in namespace

According to newuidmap man page, it requires that the caller is the owner of the process indicated by the pid. But here, the following message shows it is not the case:

   newuidmap: Target process 15777 is owned by a different user: uid:500 pw_uid:500 st_uid:0, gid:500 pw_gid:500 st_gid:0

Looking at newuidmap's code, printed value for st_uid is effective UID, and st_gid is effective GID. So it looks like runas should be called with "-u 500 -g 500 -v 0 -j 0".

Patch Set 5:

> it looks like runas should be called with "-u 500 -g 500 -v 0 -j 0"

The main confusion I have is why this is failing consistently for my patch, but not without my patch.  I didn't change how newuidmap is called, and (AFAICS) only changed how PID is collected.

parents.subject: LU-16159 osp: destroy should not overtake writes

commit.subject: LU-16457 tests: wait for remote sleep in sanity-pcc/101a
commit.message:
LU-16457 tests: wait for remote sleep in sanity-pcc/101a

Wait longer for the remote sleep command to start on the agent node.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-pcc env=ONLY=101a,ONLY_REPEAT=200
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I5dcbd6a7127b3e17aa658c87f5c75874432dc353
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/49587
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Feng Lei <flei@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
